name: International Building Code 2021
copyright_notice: International Building Code® 2021 © 2021 International Code Council, Inc. Section numbers and numeric requirements are referenced as facts; no normative text is reproduced without paraphrase.

sprinklered.label: Sprinklered (NFPA 13)
sprinklered.description: Is the building protected throughout by an NFPA 13 sprinkler system? Affects allowable height/area, travel distance, and many other thresholds.

high_rise.label: High-rise (>75 ft above lowest fire-department vehicle access)

rules.label: Egress Width – Minimum Component Width
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1005.1 – The minimum clear width of a means of egress component shall not be less than 28 in. Corridors serving occupant loads >10 shall be not less than 44 in.
rules.remediation: Widen egress components to the required minimums: corridors ≥ 44 in., stairs ≥ 44 in., and all other egress paths ≥ 28 in. clear width.

rules.label: Number of Exits Required
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1006.2 – Occupant loads 1-49: 1 exit; 50-500: 2 exits; 501-1000: 3 exits; >1000: 4 exits.
rules.remediation: Provide the minimum required number of exits for the occupant load. Verify that exits are remote from one another per §1007.1.

rules.label: Occupant Load Calculation
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1004.1 – Occupant load shall be determined by dividing the floor area by the occupant load factor for the use in Table 1004.1.2.
rules.remediation: Recalculate occupant load using the correct IBC Table 1004.1.2 load factor for each space type. Verify each space's area and intended use.

rules.label: Stair Tread & Riser Dimensions
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1011.5.2 – Risers: 4–7 in. Treads: min 11 in. Per §1011.5.3, greatest riser height shall not exceed smallest by more than 3/8 in.
rules.remediation: Adjust stair risers to 4–7 in. and treads to minimum 11 in. Ensure uniformity: no riser variance exceeding 3/8 in. between any two risers in a stair flight.

rules.label: Stair Handrails
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1011.11 – Handrails shall be provided on both sides of stairways ≥ 44 in. wide. Handrail height: 34–38 in. above stair tread nosing.
rules.remediation: Install handrails on both sides of stairs ≥ 44 in. wide at a height of 34–38 in. measured vertically from the tread nosing.

rules.label: Fire Separation Distance
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §705.5 – Exterior walls with fire separation distance < 5 ft shall have fire-resistance rating ≥ 1 hr. Distance < 3 ft requires 100% protection of openings.
rules.remediation: Verify fire separation distance to property lines and adjacent buildings. Provide required fire-resistance ratings for exterior walls and protect or eliminate openings as required.

rules.label: Automatic Sprinkler System
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §903.2 – Automatic fire sprinkler systems are required in: buildings >5,000 sq ft in Group A, all high-rise buildings, and various other occupancy types per §903.2.1–903.2.11.
rules.remediation: Install an NFPA 13 automatic fire sprinkler system throughout the building. Coordinate sprinkler coverage with architectural features and obtain fire department approval.

rules.label: Door Clear Width
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1010.1.1 – Egress doors shall provide a clear width of not less than 32 in. when set at 90°. Means of egress doors serving occupant loads >50 shall have a minimum width of 36 in.
rules.remediation: Replace or rehang egress doors to achieve minimum 32 in. clear width (36 in. for high-occupancy spaces). Clear width is measured from face of door to face of stop.

rules.label: Corridor Width
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1020.2 – Corridors shall have a minimum width of 44 in. In healthcare occupancies (Groups I-1, I-2), corridors shall be min 96 in. wide.
rules.remediation: Widen all egress corridors to minimum 44 in. clear width (measured from face of wall to face of wall, excluding baseboards and trim).

rules.label: Exit Signs Required
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1013.1 – Exits and exit access doors serving an occupant load >49 shall be marked by an approved exit sign.
rules.remediation: Install illuminated exit signs at each exit door and at each corridor junction where the direction to the nearest exit is not apparent.

rules.label: Emergency Egress Lighting
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1008.1 – The means of egress illumination level shall not be less than 1 footcandle at the walking surface. Emergency lighting shall maintain 1.5-hr illumination upon loss of power.
rules.remediation: Provide emergency egress lighting at all required means of egress paths. Install battery-backup luminaires capable of maintaining 1 fc for 90 minutes at floor level.

rules.label: Minimum Plumbing Fixtures
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §2902.1 – Minimum number of plumbing fixtures shall be provided per Table 2902.1. Separate toilet facilities for each sex required where occupant load > 15.
rules.remediation: Provide minimum plumbing fixtures per IBC Table 2902.1. Verify fixture count for each sex separately, accounting for occupant load.

rules.label: Natural Ventilation
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1203.1 – Natural ventilation openings shall be ≥ 4% of floor area of the room or space.
rules.remediation: Provide openable windows or other natural ventilation openings totaling at least 4% of the floor area served, or provide mechanical ventilation per IBC §1203.2.

rules.label: Spaces with One Exit – Maximum Occupant Load & Common Path
rules.notes_template: Verify single-exit space limits per IBC Table 1006.2.1: max ~{{params.max_occupants}} occupants and common path of egress travel ≤ {{params.max_common_path_ft}} ft.
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1006.2.1 / Table 1006.2.1 – Maximum occupant load and common path of egress travel for spaces served by a single means of egress, with values that vary by occupancy and sprinkler protection.
rules.remediation: Confirm each space's occupant load and the longest common path of egress travel are within the limits in IBC Table 1006.2.1. Add a second exit or sprinkler protection if either limit is exceeded.

rules.label: Maximum Exit Access Travel Distance
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1017.2 / Table 1017.2 – Maximum exit access travel distance, varying by occupancy and sprinkler protection.
rules.remediation: Verify the longest travel distance from any point in the space to the nearest exit does not exceed Table 1017.2 limits. Add exits, install sprinklers, or reduce floor area to comply.

rules.label: Accessible Means of Egress
rules.notes_template: Verify ≥ 2 accessible means of egress from each accessible space (1 permitted only where one exit is permitted by §1006.2). Coordinate with elevators, areas of refuge, and exit stairs.
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1009.1 – Accessible spaces shall be provided with not less than two accessible means of egress; one is permitted only where the space is served by a single exit per §1006.2.
rules.remediation: Provide two accessible means of egress from each accessible space. Where stairs are required, provide an area of refuge or use a sprinklered building exception per §1009.3.

rules.label: Assembly Occupancy Egress Capacity
rules.notes_template: Verify assembly egress: stair capacity 0.3 in/person and level capacity 0.2 in/person; aisle widths ≥ 36 in. (or 48 in. for outdoor stadium aisles); two MOE for >50 occupants.
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1029 – Assembly occupancy egress requirements include increased egress capacity factors, aisle width minimums, and main exit width requirements.
rules.remediation: Recalculate egress capacity using assembly factors (stair 0.3, level 0.2 in/person) and verify aisle widths and main exit sizing per §1029.

rules.label: Exterior Wall Fire-Resistance Rating by Separation Distance
rules.notes_template: Verify exterior wall ratings per IBC Table 705.5 using fire separation distance, occupancy group, and construction type.
rules.citation: IBC 2021 Table 705.5 – Exterior wall fire-resistance ratings vary by fire separation distance (X/<3 ft, 3 to <5 ft, 5 to <10 ft, 10 to <30 ft, ≥30 ft) and occupancy/construction.
rules.remediation: Use IBC Table 705.5 with the project's fire separation distance, occupancy group, and construction type to determine the required exterior wall fire-resistance rating, then specify a tested wall assembly.

rules.label: Fire Barriers – Continuity & Rating
rules.notes_template: Verify fire barriers are continuous from floor/ceiling to floor/roof above with the rating required by Table 707.3.10 (1, 2, 3, or 4 hr depending on the function).
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §707 / Table 707.3.10 – Fire barriers separate occupancies, exit enclosures, exit passageways, horizontal exits, and other functions; ratings 1–4 hr.
rules.remediation: Specify fire barrier walls with the rating required by Table 707.3.10, with continuity to the floor/roof deck above and properly rated penetrations and joints (§714, §715).

rules.label: Opening Protectives – Doors & Windows
rules.notes_template: Verify opening protective ratings per Table 716.1(2): fire door and fire window assemblies must match the fire-resistance rating of the wall they penetrate (per §716.1.2.1).
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §716 / Table 716.1(2) – Opening protectives in fire-resistance-rated walls must have ratings per §716 and be installed per NFPA 80.
rules.remediation: Specify labeled fire door and frame assemblies (with self- or automatic-closing hardware and positive latching) with ratings matching Table 716.1(2). Reference NFPA 80 for installation.

rules.label: Allowable Height & Area
rules.notes_template: Verify allowable height (Table 504.3), allowable stories (Table 504.4), and allowable area (Table 506.2) for the project's construction type and occupancy. Apply sprinkler increases (§504.4, §506.3) and frontage increases (§506.3.3) as applicable.
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §504 (Building Height), §505 (Mezzanines/Equipment Platforms), §506 (Building Area) – Allowable height in feet, stories, and area per floor are determined from Tables 504.3, 504.4, and 506.2.
rules.remediation: Cross-check the project height (ft and stories) and per-story area against Tables 504.3/504.4/506.2 with applicable sprinkler/frontage increases. Reduce footprint, increase construction type, or add fire walls (§706) if exceeded.

rules.label: High-Rise Building Provisions
rules.notes_template: High-rise (height > 75 ft above lowest level of fire department vehicle access). Required: NFPA 13 sprinklers, voice/alarm system, fire-fighter elevators per §3007 or §3008, smoke control (§909), standby power, two-way radio coverage.
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §403 – Buildings with an occupied floor more than 75 ft above the lowest level of fire department vehicle access are high-rises and must comply with §403's expanded fire protection, smoke control, and emergency power requirements.
rules.remediation: Provide all high-rise systems required by §403: NFPA 13 sprinklers throughout, NFPA 72 voice/alarm system, fire-service-access and occupant-evacuation elevators (§3007/§3008), §909 smoke control, standby/emergency power, and two-way radio enhancement per §510 of the IFC.

rules.label: Safety Glazing in Hazardous Locations
rules.notes_template: Verify safety (tempered/laminated) glazing at all §2406.4 hazardous locations: glazing in doors, sidelights within 24 in. of door edges, glazing within 60 in. of stair tread/landing, glazing in tubs/showers, glazing in railings, etc.
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §2406 – Glazing in hazardous locations (doors, near doors, near walking surfaces below 18 in., near stairs, in railings, in wet areas) shall be safety glazing meeting CPSC 16 CFR 1201 or ANSI Z97.1.
rules.remediation: Identify all glazing in §2406.4 hazardous locations on the drawings and specify CPSC 16 CFR 1201 Cat I/II safety glazing as required. Show the 'tempered' or 'laminated' callout adjacent to each lite.

rules.label: Elevators & Platform Lifts
rules.notes_template: Verify elevators per ASME A17.1, accessibility per §1009 / ADA Ch. 4, and where required: fire-service-access (§3007) or occupant-evacuation elevators (§3008) for high-rise.
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §3001 (general), §3002 (hoistway enclosures), §3007 (fire-service-access), §3008 (occupant-evacuation), §3009 (private residence) – elevators must comply with ASME A17.1 and applicable IBC special-purpose elevator requirements.
rules.remediation: Reference ASME A17.1/CSA B44 for elevator design and IBC §§3001–3008 for fire-protection and accessibility requirements. Confirm fire-rated hoistway enclosures, machine room access, and shaft venting.

rules.label: Interior Lighting – Habitable Spaces
rules.notes_template: Verify habitable rooms have natural light (≥ 8% floor area in glazing) or artificial lighting providing ≥ 10 fc at 30 in. above the floor.
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1204 – Habitable rooms shall be provided with natural light (8% glazing) or artificial illumination (10 fc minimum at 30 in. AFF).
rules.remediation: Demonstrate compliant glazing area or specify lighting fixtures and a photometric layout achieving ≥ 10 fc at 30 in. AFF.

rules.label: Live Loads
rules.notes_template: Verify minimum uniformly distributed live loads from Table 1607.1 are applied per occupancy/use (e.g., office 50 psf + 15 psf partition allowance, assembly fixed seats 60 psf, retail first floor 100 psf).
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1607 / Table 1607.1 – Minimum uniformly distributed and concentrated live loads by occupancy/use.
rules.remediation: Confirm structural calculations apply Table 1607.1 minimums for each occupied use, including any partition allowances and concentrated loads.

rules.label: Snow Loads
rules.notes_template: Verify ground snow load (Pg) from Figure 1608.2 or local jurisdiction; design per ASCE 7 Chapter 7 with appropriate exposure, thermal, and importance factors.
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1608 – Snow loads shall be determined per ASCE 7 using ground snow load Pg from Figure 1608.2 or as adopted by the AHJ.
rules.remediation: Use site-specific ground snow load (Pg) and ASCE 7 Chapter 7 to compute flat-roof, sloped-roof, drift, and unbalanced snow loads.

rules.label: Earthquake Loads – Seismic Design
rules.notes_template: Verify seismic design per ASCE 7 Chapters 11–23: site class, Ss/S1, design spectral accelerations, Risk Category, and Seismic Design Category (SDC) drive the lateral system selection and detailing requirements.
rules.citation: IBC 2021 §1613 – Earthquake loads shall be determined in accordance with ASCE 7. SDC governs height limits, system selection, and detailing.
rules.remediation: Document Risk Category, Ss/S1 from USGS, site class, design accelerations Sds/Sd1, and SDC. Use a permitted seismic-force-resisting system from ASCE 7 Table 12.2-1 with required detailing.

rules.label: Minimum Plumbing Fixtures – Detailed Ratios
rules.notes_template: Verify fixture counts (water closets, lavatories, drinking fountains, service sinks) per IBC Table 2902.1 by occupancy group, with occupant load split 50/50 male/female unless documented otherwise.
rules.citation: IBC 2021 Table 2902.1 – Minimum number of required plumbing fixtures by occupancy classification (e.g., A-2 1 WC per 75 ♂/40 ♀; B 1 WC per 25/25 first 50 then 1 per 50; M 1 WC per 500; etc.).
rules.remediation: Build a fixture-count schedule from Table 2902.1 using the project's occupancy and assumed/documented gender split. Provide drinking fountains (high+low) and at least one service sink per §2903.
